trivia_bullets.text: Expedition Envoy is from Battle for Zendikar (released 2015-10-02), the first set of the Zendikar vs. Eldrazi block. The set depicts Zendikar's mortal factions — including Kor, Merfolk, Humans, Elves, and Vampires — mounting a desperate resistance against the awakened Eldrazi titans Ulamog and Kozilek. Expedition houses are the organized adventuring guilds that explored Zendikar's ever-shifting terrain; the Eldrazi's emergence transformed their mission from treasure-hunting to survival. [Scryfall; card flavor text: "The unofficial motto of every expedition house is 'ready for anything,' a phrase whose significance has been amplified since the emergence of the Eldrazi."]

Type line: Creature — Human Scout Ally. The Ally subtype is mechanically load-bearing in BFZ — Allies trigger rally abilities when other Allies enter the battlefield. Expedition Envoy has no oracle text (it is a vanilla 2/1 for one white mana), meaning it contributes solely as an Ally trigger and aggressive body. ChannelFireball's BFZ constructed review noted it as a reliable early attacker in Ally-tribal aggro and White Weenie, rating it useful if the Ally theme cohered in Standard. [Scryfall; ChannelFireball BFZ White Constructed Review, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: ChannelFireball BFZ Constructed Review White via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: First and only printing (no reprints as of Scryfall records). Artist: David Palumbo. EDHREC rank 17976, reflecting minimal Commander play — vanilla 2/1s rarely earn Commander slots even in Ally tribal builds, where the rally trigger count matters more than the individual body's stats. [Scryfall]

Flavor text: "The unofficial motto of every expedition house is 'ready for anything,' a phrase whose significance has been amplified since the emergence of the Eldrazi." No speaker is named — the flavor text is written as anonymous faction doctrine, reflecting the collective voice of expedition culture rather than any single character. [Scryfall]
